questions.q: In which decade was the Indian filmmaker V. Shantaram born?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was born on 18 November 1901, placing his birth in the 1900s.

questions.q: What was the primary profession for which V. Shantaram is best remembered in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram is chiefly remembered as a pioneering film director and producer, though he also acted.

questions.q: Which film company did V. Shantaram help co-found in 1929?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram co-founded the Prabhat Film Company in 1929, along with partners including V. G. Damle and S. Fattelal.

questions.q: Which production company did V. Shantaram establish in 1942 after leaving Prabhat?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram founded Rajkamal Kalamandir in Bombay in 1942 after leaving the Prabhat Film Company.

questions.q: India's highest film honour, awarded to V. Shantaram in 1985, is named after which pioneer?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, named after the father of Indian cinema, in 1985.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's 1957 film 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' centres on the reform of which group of people?
questions.explanation: 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' follows a jailer who rehabilitates six hardened convicts through honest farm labour.

questions.q: The films of V. Shantaram are most strongly associated with which kind of cinema?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram is remembered as a pioneer of reformist social cinema in India.

questions.q: In which Indian state lies Kolhapur, the town where V. Shantaram was born?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was born in Kolhapur, in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Besides directing and producing, in which other film-craft role did V. Shantaram also work during his career?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was an actor as well as a director and producer, appearing in several early films.

questions.q: V. Shantaram passed away in which year?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram died on 30 October 1990 in Bombay.

questions.q: Which 1937 V. Shantaram film, dealing with the issue of unequal-age marriage, is regarded as one of his landmark reformist works?
questions.explanation: 'Duniya Na Mane' (1937), the Hindi version of the Marathi 'Kunku', confronts the marriage of a young woman to a much older man.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's full name was Shantaram Rajaram Vankudre. Within that name, 'Vankudre' was his:
questions.explanation: His full name was Shantaram Rajaram Vankudre, in which Vankudre was the family surname and Shantaram his given name.

questions.q: Which film was the first feature produced by V. Shantaram's Prabhat Film Company, released in 1929?
questions.explanation: 'Gopal Krishna' (1929) was the Prabhat Film Company's first feature, directed by V. Shantaram in the silent era.

questions.q: Which 1955 V. Shantaram film, built around Indian classical dance, starred Sandhya and Gopi Krishna?
questions.explanation: 'Jhanak Jhanak Payal Baaje' (1955) was V. Shantaram's classical-dance colour film starring Sandhya and dancer Gopi Krishna.

questions.q: Which classical-dance-themed V. Shantaram film of 1955 won the Filmfare Award for Best Film?
questions.explanation: 'Jhanak Jhanak Payal Baaje' (1955) won the Filmfare Award for Best Film, celebrating Indian classical dance.

questions.q: Which actress, who later married V. Shantaram, starred as the female lead in 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' (1957)?
questions.explanation: Sandhya was the leading lady of 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' and a frequent collaborator who married V. Shantaram.

questions.q: The famous prayer song 'Aye Malik Tere Bande Hum' appears in which V. Shantaram film?
questions.explanation: 'Aye Malik Tere Bande Hum' is the celebrated prayer song from V. Shantaram's 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' (1957).

questions.q: V. Shantaram's 1959 colour film featuring the song 'Aadha Hai Chandrama Raat Aadhi' was titled:
questions.explanation: 'Navrang' (1959), starring Sandhya, features the popular song 'Aadha Hai Chandrama Raat Aadhi'.

questions.q: In which decade did V. Shantaram begin his film career, working in the silent-film period before sound arrived?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram entered films in the early 1920s during the silent era, before Indian talkies began in 1931.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's Prabhat Film Company relocated in 1933 to which city, where its studio campus later became the FTII?
questions.explanation: Prabhat moved to Pune (then Poona) in 1933; its studio grounds later became the Film and Television Institute of India.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's Prabhat film 'Shejari' (Hindi 'Padosi'), released in 1941, told a story promoting which theme?
questions.explanation: 'Shejari' / 'Padosi' (1941) dramatised the friendship between a Hindu and a Muslim neighbour, promoting communal harmony.

questions.q: Which V. Shantaram film of 1936, starring Durga Khote, was screened at the Venice Film Festival?
questions.explanation: 'Amar Jyoti' (1936), starring Durga Khote as a rebel queen, was a V. Shantaram film screened at the Venice Film Festival.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's 1934 Prabhat film 'Amrit Manthan' is noted for being the first film produced at which studio location?
questions.explanation: 'Amrit Manthan' (1934) was the first film made at Prabhat's newly built studio in Pune after the company moved there in 1933.

questions.q: 'Dharmatma' (1935), a V. Shantaram film about the saint-reformer Eknath, ran into censorship trouble over its depiction of which social theme?
questions.explanation: 'Dharmatma' (1935), based on Sant Eknath, faced censorship for its treatment of untouchability and caste reform.

questions.q: 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' (1957) earned international recognition by winning a Silver Bear at which film festival?
questions.explanation: 'Do Aankhen Barah Haath' won a Silver Bear at the 1958 Berlin International Film Festival, along with a Golden Globe Samuel Goldwyn Award.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's 1951 Marathi film about the poet-composer Honaji Bala, featuring the lavani tradition, was titled:
questions.explanation: 'Amar Bhoopali' (1951) was V. Shantaram's film built around the Marathi poet-composer Honaji Bala.

questions.q: Which 1964 V. Shantaram film told its story largely through dance, music and gesture rather than conventional dialogue?
questions.explanation: 'Geet Gaya Pattharon Ne' (1964) was a V. Shantaram production told largely through dance, music and gesture, and it introduced his daughter Rajshree.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's daughter, an actress who debuted in his film 'Geet Gaya Pattharon Ne', was named:
questions.explanation: Rajshree, V. Shantaram's daughter, was an actress who debuted in 'Geet Gaya Pattharon Ne' (1964).

questions.q: V. Shantaram's 1932 Prabhat film 'Ayodhyecha Raja' is historically significant as the first film made in which language?
questions.explanation: 'Ayodhyecha Raja' (1932), directed by V. Shantaram, is regarded as the first Marathi-language talkie.

questions.q: V. Shantaram's reformist social outlook in films like 'Duniya Na Mane' aligned him most closely with which broader trend in 1930s Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: V. Shantaram was a leading figure of socially conscious, progressive reformist filmmaking in 1930s India.
